verses.text: Our fathers passed the sea and drank of 'that spiritual Rock... and that Rock was Christ.' Christ with them. He fed Israel of old as He feeds us.
Our fathers passed the sea and drank of 'that spiritual Rock... and that Rock was Christ.' Christ with them. He fed Israel of old as He feeds us.
Our fathers passed the sea and drank of 'that spiritual Rock... and that Rock was Christ.' Christ with them. He fed Israel of old as He feeds us.
Our fathers passed the sea and drank of 'that spiritual Rock... and that Rock was Christ.' Christ with them. He fed Israel of old as He feeds us.
With many God was not pleased; 'these things were our examples,' that we lust not after evil. A warning. Their fall instructs the church.
With many God was not pleased; 'these things were our examples,' that we lust not after evil. A warning. Their fall instructs the church.
With many God was not pleased; 'these things were our examples,' that we lust not after evil. A warning. Their fall instructs the church.
Neither commit fornication, nor tempt Christ, nor murmur, as some did and perished. Solemn lessons. Their sins and dooms are set before us.
Neither commit fornication, nor tempt Christ, nor murmur, as some did and perished. Solemn lessons. Their sins and dooms are set before us.
Neither commit fornication, nor tempt Christ, nor murmur, as some did and perished. Solemn lessons. Their sins and dooms are set before us.
These are written for our admonition; 'let him that thinketh he standeth take heed lest he fall.' Watch. Confidence in self goes before a fall, yet God makes a way to escape.
These are written for our admonition; 'let him that thinketh he standeth take heed lest he fall.' Watch. Confidence in self goes before a fall, yet God makes a way to escape.
These are written for our admonition; 'let him that thinketh he standeth take heed lest he fall.' Watch. Confidence in self goes before a fall, yet God makes a way to escape.
Flee from idolatry; I speak to wise men, judge ye. A plain charge. Wisdom shuns all idol-worship.
Flee from idolatry; I speak to wise men, judge ye. A plain charge. Wisdom shuns all idol-worship.
The cup and bread are 'the communion of the blood... and body of Christ.' Holy fellowship. The supper unites us with Christ and one another.
The cup and bread are 'the communion of the blood... and body of Christ.' Holy fellowship. The supper unites us with Christ and one another.
What the Gentiles sacrifice 'they sacrifice to devils'; I would not have you in fellowship with devils. No mixing. The Lord's table and the idol's cannot be joined.
What the Gentiles sacrifice 'they sacrifice to devils'; I would not have you in fellowship with devils. No mixing. The Lord's table and the idol's cannot be joined.
What the Gentiles sacrifice 'they sacrifice to devils'; I would not have you in fellowship with devils. No mixing. The Lord's table and the idol's cannot be joined.
'Ye cannot drink the cup of the Lord, and the cup of devils.' Exclusive Lord. To share with idols provokes God to jealousy.
'Ye cannot drink the cup of the Lord, and the cup of devils.' Exclusive Lord. To share with idols provokes God to jealousy.
All things lawful are not expedient; 'let no man seek his own, but every man another's.' Mind others. Liberty bows to the good of the brethren.
All things lawful are not expedient; 'let no man seek his own, but every man another's.' Mind others. Liberty bows to the good of the brethren.
Eat what is sold, 'asking no question for conscience sake.' Free conscience. The believer need not fret over every meal.
Eat what is sold, 'asking no question for conscience sake.' Free conscience. The believer need not fret over every meal.
Eat what is sold, 'asking no question for conscience sake.' Free conscience. The believer need not fret over every meal.
But if told it was offered to an idol, eat not, for the other's conscience. Yield for love. Liberty gives way to another's scruple.
But if told it was offered to an idol, eat not, for the other's conscience. Yield for love. Liberty gives way to another's scruple.
But if told it was offered to an idol, eat not, for the other's conscience. Yield for love. Liberty gives way to another's scruple.
'Whether... ye eat, or drink... do all to the glory of God'; give none offence. The grand rule. Let everything aim at God's glory and others' good.
'Whether... ye eat, or drink... do all to the glory of God'; give none offence. The grand rule. Let everything aim at God's glory and others' good.
